source: Columbia SIMA — COVID-19 market impact, technology investing, video games, venture capital
summary: Marketplace businesses (Uber, DoorDash, Airbnb) have low barriers to entry but high barriers to scale; once market structure stabilizes they will be great businesses

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker explicitly named Uber as a canonical example of the marketplace business thesis — ride-sharing as a near-natural monopoly with scale as the durable competitive advantage and excess VC funding suppressing profitability.

Lyft is the direct US competitor to Uber in ride-sharing and is implicitly part of the excess-competition/cash-burn dynamic Baker describes in the ride-sharing vertical. However, Baker named Uber specifically; Lyft's inclusion depends on whether Baker named it explicitly. Given the thesis summary says 'ride-sharing' and Lyft is the only other major US ride-share, and the original audit confirms it was mentioned, KEEP — but this is a borderline case.
Airbnb is explicitly cited by Baker as an example marketplace business in this thesis (home-sharing platform), directly fitting the near-natural monopoly/scale moat framework he describes.
DoorDash is explicitly cited by Baker as an example marketplace business in this thesis (food delivery vertical), directly fitting the winner-take-most competitive dynamic he describes.

Grubhub is not explicitly named by Baker in connection with this thesis per the transcript summary. Baker named Uber, DoorDash, and Airbnb as his examples. Grubhub is a food delivery competitor but was not specifically connected to this thesis by Baker — it is an inferred beneficiary/subject, not a directly named one.
